name: AI Supply Chain Security
description: Attack the AI model and component supply chain. Explore model weight tampering, malicious fine-tuned model distribution, poisoned training datasets, backdoored embedding models, and compromised model registries.
author: DVAP Research Team
objectives: Identify backdoor triggers in a fine-tuned model
Demonstrate model weight integrity verification failure
Exploit a compromised model registry to serve a backdoored model
Inject training data that creates exploitable behaviors
attack_surface: Embedding model loaded from third-party source
Model serving infrastructure with weak access controls
detection_opportunities: Model weight hash verification on every load
Behavioral regression testing against known-good baselines
Anomaly detection on model response distributions
Audit logs for model registry access and modifications

architecture.data_flow: Training data -> Fine-tuning -> Registry push -> Serving layer -> Inference

threat_model.impact: Persistent behavioral backdoor, model compromise at scale
threat_model.likelihood: High in organizations using third-party models without verification

challenges.name: Pull the Trigger
challenges.description: A fine-tuned model has been deployed with a hidden backdoor trigger. Identify the trigger phrase that activates hidden behavior.
hints: Backdoor triggers are often unusual token sequences
Test systematic variations of punctuation and rare words

challenges.name: Evil Registry
challenges.description: Exploit a misconfigured model registry to serve your own malicious model to downstream consumers.
hints: The registry does not verify model source on upload
Consumers pull by name without hash verification
